turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <rect width="512" height="512" fill="#ffffff"/>

  <!-- Sky/Background gradient -->
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="g" x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#bfe3ff"/>
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#eef7ff"/>
    </linearGradient>
  </defs>
  <rect x="0" y="0" width="512" height="340" fill="url(#g)"/>

  <!-- Road -->
  <path d="M140 500 L230 190 Q245 150 270 150 L290 150 Q315 150 330 190 L420 500 Z" fill="#2f2f35"/>
  <path d="M220 500 L300 200" stroke="#f6c12a" stroke-width="10" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.95"/>

  <!-- Dashed lane markings -->
  <g opacity="0.95">
    <path d="M235 420 L265 300" stroke="#ffd966" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round"/>
    <path d="M220 450 L250 330" stroke="#ffd966" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round"/>
    <path d="M250 390 L285 260" stroke="#ffd966" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Car body (side/front perspective) -->
  <g transform="translate(0,0)">
    <!-- shadow on road -->
    <ellipse cx="270" cy="355" rx="95" ry="22" fill="#000" opacity="0.12"/>

    <!-- body -->
    <path d="M200 320
             Q215 290 250 280
             L285 270
             Q300 260 320 265
             Q355 275 380 290
             Q402 304 405 330
             L408 352
             Q410 370 395 372
             L215 372
             Q190 370 190 350
             Q190 335 200 320 Z" 
          fill="#4f7cff" stroke="#2b3b6e" stroke-width="3"/>

    <!-- windshield -->
    <path d="M260 292
             Q270 270 295 266
             Q320 261 350 276
             Q355 279 357 287
             Q360 300 348 306
             L275 318
             Q258 318 260 292 Z" 
          fill="#9ad7ff" stroke="#2b3b6e" stroke-width="3"/>

    <!-- hood/trim -->
    <path d="M210 336
             Q245 320 290 312
             Q340 304 390 330" 
          fill="none" stroke="#d9ecff" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.9"/>

    <!-- front bumper/stripe -->
    <path d="M360 362 Q382 360 397 353" fill="none" stroke="#ffd966" stroke-width="8" stroke-linecap="round"/>

    <!-- wheels -->
    <g>
      <circle cx="238" cy="372" r="30" fill="#1b1b22" stroke="#0a0a0f" stroke-width="4"/>
      <circle cx="238" cy="372" r="14" fill="#3a3a45" stroke="#0a0a0f" stroke-width="3"/>
      <circle cx="370" cy="372" r="32" fill="#1b1b22" stroke="#0a0a0f" stroke-width="4"/>
      <circle cx="370" cy="372" r="15" fill="#3a3a45" stroke="#0a0a0f" stroke-width="3"/>
    </g>

    <!-- axle/underbody -->
    <path d="M205 372 Q242 360 275 366" stroke="#121218" stroke-width="8" stroke-linecap="round"/>
    <path d="M275 366 Q315 360 405 352" stroke="#121218" stroke-width="8" stroke-linecap="round"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Motion lines to suggest driving -->
  <g opacity="0.9" stroke="#ffffff" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round">
    <path d="M120 420 Q165 390 215 388" fill="none"/>
    <path d="M130 445 Q175 415 225 413" fill="none"/>
    <path d="M140 470 Q190 445 245 443" fill="none"/>
  </g>
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.reasoning:
**Designing a visual concept**

I need to create a visual representation of "drive" without using letters or text. My idea is to represent it as a one-word game, perhaps by drawing a car driving on a road. I’ll focus on simple shapes, like using a road and wheels to convey the concept. After that, I’ll return a JSON object that includes an SVG string for the drawing. This should keep it clear and fun!

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

content.text: {"language":"EN-US","maxHints":20,"note":"Return 20 ordered guesses. Position 1 is the highest-confidence guess."}

message.reasoning:
**Interpreting the image**

I’m taking a closer look at this cartoon-like image. It kind of resembles a miner, but then again, there’s a black cone in the center that looks like a mountain with blue goggles and headlights. Is that a penguin? No, it seems like a person in a helmet. I initially thought it could be an astronaut or even a ninja, but the yellow stripes add a twist. Perhaps it's meant to represent a volcano or a baseball cap? Hm… quite puzzling!**Identifying the character**

I'm puzzling over this character that looks a bit like a helmet or hood with goggles. I wonder if it's a robot or something like a sailor, but I'm not quite sure. The black hood could represent a conductor or maybe even something cheesy? It seems most likely to be an object like "hood," "hat," or "penguin." It looks like a penguin wearing a helmet, yet there's no beak. The shapes and colors are throwing me off a bit! Could it resemble a turtle or perhaps a dinosaur?**Guessing character identity**

I’m considering the character features and thinking it might be a "penguin" because of the black body and blue area. I’m listing my best guesses here: penguin, scuba, astronaut, helmet, goggles, ninja, hood, mask, robot, miner, sailor, diver, safety, visor, and submarine. I need to come up with about 20 unique one-word options, arranging them by likelihood. I think "penguin" is my best guess, followed by "goggles." Let's get this list sorted out!
